verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is a mitochondrial-matrix chaperonin with no intrinsic membrane anchor (TM, GPI, or lipidation). However, it is a well-documented ecto-chaperone that translocates to the cell surface during stress (heat shock, oxidative stress, ER stress, hypoxia, nutrient deprivation), in dying cells (apoptosis), and on tumor cells where it functions as a DAMP and tumor-associated antigen. The surface pool is transient and stress-dependent, qualifying as cell_state_induced. No documented stable surface attachment or minority PM pool at baseline.
